title: श्री यमुना स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री यमुना स्तोत्रम् ॥

नमोऽस्तु यमुने सदा तव चरित्रमत्यद्भुतं
न जातु मम विस्मृतिस्त्वयि यथा भवेत्सुन्दरि।
यमोपगृहनिर्गतां भुवि जनार्तिहन्त्रीं सतां
नमामि शिरसा सदा भवभयापहां नर्मदाम्॥१॥

माता यशोदया शिशुः स्तनयुगं यत्रापिबत्केशवः
कृष्णस्य भगवान्स्वयं जलकणैर्यस्याः स्पृशन्नासनम्।
तामेव यमुनां नमामि सततं स्मेरामृतस्रोतसं
कालिन्दीं कलुषापहां मुनिगणैः सेव्यां सदा सेवये॥२॥

कृष्णेन सहरासलीलनिरतां गोपीजनैरावृतां
गोवर्धनगिरेः समीपसरितं पुण्यां मनोहारिणीम्।
कालिन्दतनयां पवित्रसलिलां श्यामां सुशोभास्पदं
श्रीयमुनां नमामि सततं श्रीकृष्णजीवातुकाम्॥३॥

यत्रैव नन्दनन्दनो विजहरे गोपाङ्गनाभिः सह
यत्रैव हरिणाद्यरण्यचरणाः पानं प्रचक्रुर्मुदा।
मथुरापुरनिकेतिनी सुरसरित्कालिन्दजा मङ्गला
सा मे मनसि सर्वदा विलसतु श्रीकृष्णकान्ता नदी॥४॥

स्नानेन तव वारिणा शुचि भवेद्देहं मनो निर्मलं
पापं सर्वमपैतु दूरगमनं मे कुष्ठरोगादिकम्।
गोपालेन सदानुसेवितपदे गोविन्ददत्तश्रिये
श्रीयमुने नमोऽस्तु भवतामेकान्तवत्सलप्रिये॥५॥

मातर्यमुने तवापि शरणं याचेऽहमत्यन्ततः
पापी चास्मि सदैव देवि कृपया त्रायस्व मां सर्वतः।
तत्त्वज्ञानमनन्तपुण्यमतुलं भक्तिं च विष्णोर्मम
त्वत्तीरे वसतिं प्रयच्छ यमुने सर्वार्थदे सर्वदा॥६॥

यमुनाष्टकमिदं शुभं पठति यः सदा मानवः।
सर्वपापक्षयं प्राप्य विष्णुलोकं स गच्छति॥

॥ इति श्री यमुना स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Yamuna Stotram ||

Namo'stu Yamune Sadaa Tava Charitramaty​adbhutam
Na Jaatu Mama Vismritistvay​i Yathaa Bhavetsundari.
Yamopagrihanirgataam Bhuvi Janaartihantreem Sataam
Namaami Shirasaa Sadaa Bhavabhayaapahaam.||1||

Maataa Yashodayaa Shishuh Stanayugam Yatraapibatk​eshavah
Krishnasya Bhagavaansvayam Jalakanairyasyaah Sprishannaa​sanam.
Taameva Yamunaam Namaami Satatam Smeraamritasro​tasam
Kaalindeam Kalushaaapahaam Muniganais​hevyaam Sadaa Sevaye.||2||

Krishnena Saharaasaleelanira​taam Gopeejanairaa​vritaam
Govardhanagiireh Sameepasaritam Punyaam Manohaarineem.
Kaalindatanayaam Pavitrasalilaam Shyaamaam Sushobhaaspadam
ShriYamunaam Namaami Satatam Shrikrishnajee​vaatukaam.||3||

Yatraiva Nandanandano Vijahare Gopaanganabhih Saha
Yatraiva Harinaadyaranyacharanaah Paanam Prachakrurmudaa.
Mathuraapuraniketinee Surasaritkaalindajaa Mangalaa
Saa Me Manasi Sarvadaa Vilasatu Shrikrishnaka​antaa Nadee.||4||

Snaanena Tava Vaarinaa Shuchi Bhaveddeh​am Mano Nirmalam
Paapam Sarvamapatu Dooragamanam Me Kushtharogaadi​kam.
Gopaalena Sadaanusevitapade Govindadattashriye
ShriYamune Namo'stu Bhavataamekaanta​vatsalapri​ye.||5||

Maataryamune Tavaapi Sharanam Yaache'ha​matyanta​tah
Paapee Chaasmi Sadaiva Devi Kripayaa Traayasva Maam Sarvatah.
Tattvajnaanam​anantapunyam​atulam Bhaktim Cha Vishnormama
Tvatteere Vasatim Prayachchha Yamune Sarvaarthade Sarvadaa.||6||

Yamunaashtak​amidam Shubham Pathati Yah Sadaa Maanavah.
Sarvapaapakshayam Praapya Vishnulokam Sa Gachchhati.

|| Iti Shri Yamuna Stotram Sampoornam ||
hindiMeaning:
(1) हे यमुने! तुम्हें सदा नमस्कार। तुम्हारा चरित्र अत्यन्त अद्भुत है। हे सुन्दरी! तुम्हारी कभी विस्मृति न हो। यम के गृह से निकली, भूमि पर जनों की पीड़ा हरने वाली, भवभय को नष्ट करने वाली को शिर से नमस्कार।

(2) जहाँ माता यशोदा के शिशु केशव ने स्तन-युग्म से पान किया, कृष्ण भगवान ने स्वयं जिसके जल-कणों का स्पर्श किया — उसी अमृतस्रोतस्विनी, कलुषापहा यमुना को सदा नमस्कार करता हूँ, मुनिगणों द्वारा सेव्या कालिन्दी की सेवा करता हूँ।

(3) कृष्ण के साथ रासलीला में निरत, गोपी-जनों से आवृत, गोवर्धन गिरि के समीप बहने वाली, पुण्य, मनोहारिणी, कालिन्द की पुत्री, पवित्र जल वाली, श्याम वर्ण की, सुशोभा की निवास, श्रीकृष्ण की जीवनधारा यमुना को सदा नमस्कार।

(4) जहाँ नन्दनन्दन ने गोपांगनाओं के साथ विहार किया, जहाँ हरिण आदि वनचर आनन्द से जल पिया — वह मथुरा निवासिनी, कालिन्दजा, मंगला, श्रीकृष्ण-कान्ता नदी सदा मेरे मन में विलसे।

(5) तुम्हारे जल में स्नान से देह शुचि हो, मन निर्मल हो, सब पाप दूर जाएँ, कुष्ठ रोग आदि नष्ट हों। गोपाल द्वारा सेवित, गोविन्द द्वारा श्री-प्रदत्त, एकान्तवत्सल-प्रिया यमुने! नमस्कार।

(6) हे माता यमुने! अत्यन्त शरणागत होकर याचना करता हूँ। सदैव पापी हूँ, हे देवी! कृपया सब ओर से रक्षा करो। तत्त्वज्ञान, अनन्त पुण्य, अतुल भक्ति और तुम्हारे तट पर निवास प्रदान करो। हे सर्वार्थदा यमुने!
benefits: Yamuna is Krishna's beloved river - this stotra invokes His special grace
Purifies body and mind, removes skin diseases and all sins
Invokes residence in vishnu-loka after death
Invokes the blessing of devotion to krishna and spiritual knowledge
Sacred connection to Vrindavan, Mathura, and Krishna's divine pastimes
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Krishna, Yamuna Stotram (श्री यमुना स्तोत्रम्) stands as a devotional hymn of praise. Rooted in Traditional Sanskrit Hymn, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to bestow devotion to Krishna and spiritual knowledge as well as purify body and mind, removes skin diseases and all sins. Additionally, it is known to grant residence in Vishnu-loka after death. For best results, devotees chant this on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Yamuna Chhath and Janmashtami. Comprising 6 verses, it can be completed in approximately 6 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Yamuna Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Yamuna Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred hymn to River Yamuna, beloved of Krishna, for purification, devotion and spiritual merit.

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Yamuna Stotram and what is its place in Pushti Marg?
seoFaqs.answer: The Yamuna Stotram, popularly known as Yamunashtakam, was composed by Shri Vallabhacharya (1479–1531 CE), the founder of the Pushti Marg sampradaya. It is one of his Shodasha-grantha (sixteen foundational works). The hymn consists of nine verses — eight in praise of Shri Yamuna Maharani and a ninth phala-shruti verse describing the fruits of recitation. In every Pushti Marg haveli (temple of Shrinathji and Krishna), Yamunashtakam is sung daily as part of the morning and evening seva, making it the most chanted Yamuna hymn in Vaishnavism. (Source: Pushti-marg.net Yamunashtak; Brajrasik Yamunashtakam.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is Yamuna addressed as the 'sister of Yama' in the stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: In Vedic mythology, Yamuna and Yama are twin children of the Sun god Surya and Sanjna. The Yamunashtakam (verse 6) plays on this familial bond: 'Just by drinking your water, one is exempt from suffering at the hands of Yamaraj, the lord of justice and redemption. Being his sister, how can he punish your children no matter how sinful they may be?' This makes Yamuna uniquely a 'Yama-pasha-mochana' goddess — one who, by sibling affection, dissolves the noose of death-judgment. The stotra is therefore prized at end-of-life pilgrimages to Mathura-Vrindavan. (Source: 108puzzles Yamunashtak meaning; HinduMirror Yamunashtak.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the seventh verse's promise about transforming the body?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse seven of the Yamunashtakam contains the famous prayer: 'In your proximity, may my body be divinely transformed; thus making it possible for my devotion for Krishna (Murari) to flourish, O beloved of Mukund.' Vallabhacharya identifies Yamuna as not just a river but as Krishna's eternal consort in Vraja-leela, whose physical waters are bhakti-rasa flowing in liquid form. Pushti Marg theology holds that bathing in or sipping Yamuna jal causes a deha-shuddhi (body-purification) that makes one fit for the highest grade of Krishna-bhakti. (Source: Brajrasik Vallabhacharya Yamunashtakam; Pushtikul Shodash Grantha.)

seoFaqs.question: What does the closing phala-shruti say about daily recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: The ninth verse, the phala-shruti, declares: 'This ashtaka of yours, O daughter of the sun, if recited on a regular basis in a happy mood, will destroy all bad qualities and increase the devotee's love for Lord Mukund — and Lord Murari (Krishna) will be very content.' Notice the emphasis on prasanna-chitta (cheerful disposition) — Vallabhacharya's distinctive Pushti Marg principle that grace flows through joy, not austerity. The hymn is therefore sung in raga, accompanied by tabla and harmonium, in a celebratory rather than penitent mode. (Source: Bhaktisetu Haveli Yamunashtak; Pushti-marg.net.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Yamunashtakam differ from generic river-goddess hymns like the Ganga Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: While Adi Shankaracharya's Ganga Stotram praises Ganga as Shiva's consort and Vishnu-padodbhava (born of Vishnu's foot), Vallabhacharya's Yamunashtakam is uniquely Krishna-centric — every verse positions Yamuna as Krishna's beloved (priya), the witness of his rasa-leela, and the conduit of pushti (nourishing grace). Where Ganga grants moksha through liberation, Yamuna grants prema-bhakti through participation in Vraja-leela. This distinction makes the Yamunashtakam the foundational hymn of any Vaishnava who follows the Pushti Marg path of grace-without-effort. (Source: Vibrant Gujarat Yamunastakam; India Divine Yamunashtakam.)

seoFaqs.question: Where on the Yamuna is the stotram especially chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: The most sanctified spots are the 24 Yamuna ghats of Mathura — especially Vishrama Ghat, where Krishna is said to have rested after killing Kamsa — and the 12 ghats of Vrindavan, including Keshi Ghat where he slew the horse-demon Keshi. Pushti Marg pilgrims complete the Vraja Chaurasi Kos parikrama (84-kos circumambulation of Vraja) reciting the Yamunashtakam at each Yamuna touchpoint. Govardhan-Giriraja parikrama also includes a stop at Manasi Ganga, where Yamuna is invoked through the same stotram. (Source: Pushti-marg.net pilgrimage guide; Bhaktisetu Haveli.)
